principles.name: Template Inheritance
principles.description: Agent templates in structural families must use Nunjucks extends/block composition, not flat duplication

rules.rule: Template directories where a base-*.md exists must have child templates that use {% extends %}
anti_patterns: Flat task.md in a directory that has a base-*.md template
Copy-pasting base template content into a standalone file

rules.rule: Agents in identified structural families (read→findings→schema) must inherit from the family base template, not define standalone flat templates
rules.check_pattern: Compare template structure of investigator, researcher, gap-identifier, gap-resolution-assessor — flag flat templates that share >60% structural similarity
examples: analyzers/structure.md extends analyzers/base-analyzer.md with block overrides
anti_patterns: investigator/task.md and researcher/task.md both define identical output schema sections independently

rules.rule: New agent templates must check existing base templates before creating a flat file — if a matching family exists, use extends

principles.name: Agent Input Schema Declaration
principles.description: Agents must declare their input contract explicitly — implicit input shapes are defects

rules.rule: Every .agent.yaml that receives structured input from workflow steps must define inputSchema
rules.check_pattern: for f in packages/pi-workflows/agents/*.agent.yaml; do grep -qL 'inputSchema' "$f" && echo "MISSING: $(basename $f)"; done
anti_patterns: Agent accepts {{ path }}, {{ exploration }}, {{ architecture }} in templates but declares no inputSchema

rules.rule: inputSchema fields must match the Nunjucks template variable references — no undeclared template variables
rules.check_pattern: Extract {{ var }} references from templates, compare against inputSchema properties
examples: inputSchema declares path, exploration; template uses {{ path }}, {{ exploration }}
anti_patterns: Template uses {{ gap.details }} but inputSchema has no gap property

principles.name: Parametric Agent Reuse
principles.description: Agents used across multiple workflows must accept variable input, not identical wiring

rules.rule: An agent referenced by 2+ workflow specs must accept at least one input field that varies between those workflows
rules.check_pattern: grep -r 'agent:' packages/pi-workflows/workflows/*.yaml | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -10
anti_patterns: code-explorer in 6 workflows, always with path: ${{ input.path }} and nothing else
structure-analyzer in 6 workflows, always with identical exploration + path

rules.rule: Agent templates for reused agents must include Nunjucks conditionals that adapt behavior based on optional input fields
examples: {% if depth %}Limit exploration to {{ depth }} levels{% endif %}
anti_patterns: Template has zero conditionals — same output regardless of input variation

principles.name: Agent Output Typing
principles.description: Agent steps must declare output format — untyped output is a composition defect

rules.rule: Every agent step in a workflow that feeds data to downstream steps via ${{ steps.X }} must declare output.format or output.schema
rules.check_pattern: Parse workflow YAML, find agent steps referenced in ${{ steps.X }} expressions, verify output declaration
anti_patterns: Agent step produces JSON but declares no output format — downstream steps get raw text
